glossary.definition: A parameter-efficient fine-tuning technique that freezes pre-trained model weights and injects trainable rank decomposition matrices into each layer of the Transformer architecture.

An alignment method that optimizes language models to conform to human preferences by optimizing a closed-form objective directly on pairwise preference data, bypassing reinforcement learning reward model training.
An attention algorithm that optimizes Key-Value (KV) cache memory allocation by partitioning the cache into non-contiguous physical blocks using virtual memory principles.
An architecture where a gating network dynamically routes input tokens to specific specialist feedforward networks ('experts'), scaling parameters while keeping active compute constant.

references.title: Build a Large Language Model (From Scratch)
references.author: Sebastian Raschka

research_papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
research_papers.authors: Edward J. Hu, Yibin Shen, Phillip Wallis, Zeyuan Allen-Zhu, Yuanzhi Li, Shean Wang, Lu Wang, Weizhu Chen

research_papers.title: Direct Preference Optimization: Your Language Model is Secretly a Reward Model
research_papers.authors: Rafael Rafailov, Archit Sharma, Eric Mitchell, Stefano Ermon, Christopher D. Manning, Chelsea Finn

research_papers.title: PagedAttention: Efficient Memory Management for Large Language Model Serving with vLLM
research_papers.authors: Woosuk Kwon, Zhuohan Li, Siyuan Zhuang, Sheng Shen, Joseph E. Gonzalez, Ken Lahanas, Ion Stoica
